mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP combined with rising consumer credit balances signals expanding aggregate demand, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no positive correlation with rising GDPC1 and RSAFS trends across a 5-year walk-forward test (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent indicates a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, leading to higher equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain flat or negative when UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent in more than 40 percent of observed instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and housing starts reflects robust capital expenditure and construction activity, signaling healthy supply-side momentum that boosts industrial and financial sector profits.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of cases over a 4-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates coupled with accelerating CPI inflation compress equity risk premiums and increase discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns during active tightening phases.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL both rise, invalidating the discount rate compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in total nonfarm payrolls serves as a high-frequency leading indicator for near-term economic momentum, driving immediate positive repricing of equity cash flows over the next week.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns show no statistically significant positive relationship with PAYEMS acceleration over a 2-year rolling window (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals weakening consumer confidence and credit availability, which reduces durable goods demand and pressures equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5 percent over three consecutive months
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50 percent of instances following a 5 percent three-month drop in HOUST over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between slowing real GDP growth and persistently elevated federal funds rates creates a policy error scenario where tight financial conditions choke off economic expansion, triggering negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling GDPC1 and high FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 45 percent of historical episodes over a 5-year walk-forward analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production relative to historical baselines indicates improving capacity utilization and supply chain efficiency, which expands profit margins and drives positive short-term equity performance.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum does not correlate with positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of observations across a 3-year rolling evaluation.
